author: Altaf Husain Hali, Khawaja, Shamas-ul-Ulama
title: Nang-i-Khidmat
gloss: A poem in which the writer condemns the existing tendency among the Muhammadans of earning their living by means of clerkships, and advocates the learning of arts, handicrafts, trade, &c.
notes: Third Vakil Trading Hali title of the day ('The Shame of Service' — anti-clerkship, pro-trade polemic matching the Punjabi Hinduon ka Asli Khaka theme from the other side). Printer spelled 'Rozbasear' here.

author: Altaf Husain Hali, Khawaja, Shamas-ul-Ulama
title: Ta'assub wa Insaf
gloss: A poem describing the evils of bigotry and the benefits of justice.
notes: Author dash-continuation; fourth Hali title registered 27 September (regs 1246–1249 block) — the Vakil Trading Co. registering a Hali mini-canon in one transaction.

title: Bahar-i-Gulshan
gloss: A collection of Gazals or Urdu love verses by various poets, edited by M. S. Jauhar.
publisher: B. Daya Singh and Son, Book-sellers
notes: M. S. Jauhar again (dramatist-transliterator of p19) — now anthologizing ghazals; the same commercial literary operator across genres.

author: Ghulam Haidar Khan, Sardar
title: Armughan-i-Haidar
publisher: L. Divan Chand, Proprietor, Kutabkhana T. C. Gujrati
notes: Published by the Kutabkhana of T. C. Gujrati — the Lala T. C. Gujrati who authored Khalsa Dharam (p12 s269); bookshop-publisher crossover.

author: Hari Dev, Pandit
title: Jiwan Chritr Dharam Wir Haqiqat Rai
gloss: The story of the execution of the famous Hindu boy Haqiqat Rai by the Muhammadan governor of Lahore for refusing to embrace Islam.
notes: Haqiqat Rai martyrology ('Dharam Wir' — hero of the faith) at 3 pies — the mass-price end of communal memory production; compare the registrar's flat summary with the charged content.

author: Muhammad Hamid Shah
title: Shajra-i-Chishtiya Sabiriya wa Qadiriya, Razzaqia
gloss: The genealogical tables of the Chishtiya and Qadiriya orders of Sufis.
notes: Printed silsila genealogy (shajra) — Sufi lineage authority reproduced as a 6-pie pamphlet from the rival Mujaddidi order's press.
